acceptance_targets: [chapter_10_f001_curated_moral_turn] By the end of the revised material anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:141 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:187, a cold reader can state both why Rosa needs the ring and one concrete reason Carmen still should not trust or excuse it. The final beat at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:191 still ends with Carmen leaving the second count unspoken, but that silence now reads as a deliberate choice made against explicit counterpressure.

[chapter_10_f002_rosa_thesis_speech] After the revised equivalent of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:177, Rosa's remaining dialogue contains at least one concrete pressure detail and at least one sign of live speech friction such as interruption, self-correction, an unfinished turn, or a blunt refusal. It fails if the scene still ends with Rosa summarizing her entire ethical position in one polished explanatory sentence.

[chapter_10_f003_fragment_inventory_tic] Across the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:19, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:33, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:43, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:151, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:189, the money breakdown, Rosa's insurance math, and Carmen's changed med-room perception all remain legible, but no single local passage stacks three fragment-inventory beats in a row. It fails if the chapter still relies on the same clipped list cadence as its default connective rhythm.

[F06_ch10_expository_architecture] Read lines 1970-2025. (1) Carmen does something active in the scene — asks probing questions, challenges allocations, discovers information through her own reading, or makes a demand — rather than receiving a briefing. (2) The financial specifics (amounts, recipients, percentages) remain intact. (3) At least one moment of friction, disagreement, or surprise occurs during the reveal. (4) The scene does not consist of Harry speaking for more than three consecutive lines without Carmen or another character generating pressure.
[length_chapter_10_min_words] Chapter word count is >= 4000 while preserving heading contract.

findings.acceptance_test: By the end of the revised material anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:141 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:187, a cold reader can state both why Rosa needs the ring and one concrete reason Carmen still should not trust or excuse it. The final beat at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:191 still ends with Carmen leaving the second count unspoken, but that silence now reads as a deliberate choice made against explicit counterpressure.
findings.problem: The chapter's defining ethical shift is argued through a curated proof set: Harry chooses the buyer, Rosa arrives with the exact price-comparison evidence the chapter needs, and Carmen's med-room silence follows immediately. Because the scene gives too little on-page counterpressure from the harms or distortions of diversion, Carmen's deeper uptake reads staged by the chapter rather than earned through genuinely contested pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the setup at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:115, the Rosa conversation beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:141, and the aftermath beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:187 so Rosa's testimony is not the only moral proof on offer. Add one concrete friction Carmen must weigh on-page: a buyer-side lie Agnes has had to catch, a resident-side deprivation or tradeoff, an explicit acknowledgment that Rosa is the ring's best-case sample, or a sharper legal/clinical cost than the current abstract license fear. The ending at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:191 should still keep the second count unspoken, but as a compromise chosen against named counterpressure rather than the automatic conclusion of a clean demonstration.

findings.acceptance_test: After the revised equivalent of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:177, Rosa's remaining dialogue contains at least one concrete pressure detail and at least one sign of live speech friction such as interruption, self-correction, an unfinished turn, or a blunt refusal. It fails if the scene still ends with Rosa summarizing her entire ethical position in one polished explanatory sentence.
findings.problem: Rosa's closing speech turns a pressured exchange into a polished thesis recap. After "With what?" already lands the scene's economic violence, the long explanation that follows restates the chapter's whole moral argument in balanced, fully processed sentences instead of letting Rosa stay defensive, tired, or messy on the page.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the exchange beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:175 and especially Rosa's speech at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:181 so the affordability point survives without becoming a position paper. Keep her anger and practical clarity, but let the line break under pressure through interruption, self-correction, blunt refusal, or a return to concrete detail such as cost, lost work, missing care, or symptom management.

findings.acceptance_test: Across the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:19, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:33, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:43, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:151, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:189, the money breakdown, Rosa's insurance math, and Carmen's changed med-room perception all remain legible, but no single local passage stacks three fragment-inventory beats in a row. It fails if the chapter still relies on the same clipped list cadence as its default connective rhythm.
findings.problem: The prose repeatedly falls back on the same fragment-inventory scaffold: clipped noun burst, clipped noun burst, clipped verdict. In a chapter already dominated by ledgers, lists, and price math, that recurrence stops feeling like deliberate compression and starts feeling like autopilot, flattening rhythm where the chapter needs more syntactic variation.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the fragment-heavy runs beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:19, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:33, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:43, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:127, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:151, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:189. Keep the accounting texture and procedural specificity, but recast most of those beats into fuller sentences or different syntactic shapes so the clipped inventory form is reserved for only the sharpest pressure moments.

evidence.excerpt: 1984: Harry turned one of Agnes's notebooks toward her. "Jack. Five hundred. Denise Washington. Four hundred. Lola's church clinic. Three-fifty. Burt. Five hundred. Agnes. Four hundred."

2000: He tapped the first line. Jack Zielinski, his grandson, got five hundred a month toward a fifteen-thousand-dollar hole from a landscaping business in South Brunswick. Jack thought the money came from a certificate of deposit Harry had cashed before Lakeview. Harry's voice stayed level when he said the name, but his finger pressed harder against the paper.
2014: Harry turned the page. RESERVE / 650 was written at the top in Agnes's narrow block letters. Under it: MONEY ORDERS. GAS. REPLACE EXPIRED STOCK. BUYER BRIDGE. LOSS.

rewrite_direction.excerpt:
1974: He started with the money.
1975: 
1976: "Eighteen months," Harry said. "Since early last year. Gross averages four thousand a month. Forty-eight total, give or take."
1977: 
1978: Carmen had expected the number to feel larger. Spread across eighteen months and this many hands, it felt almost modest. Modest enough to ruin everyone anyway.
1979: 
[...]
2015: 
2016: "You run a contingency fund," Carmen said.
2017: 
2018: "You wanted all of it."
2019: 
2020: She looked at the ledger again. Two thousand one hundred and fifty to the people involved. Six hundred and fifty to keep the thing from seizing up. It was closer to a business than she'd wanted. More embarrassing than a business, because the books were clean.

findings.problem: The financial-architecture reveal (lines 1974-2020) is structured as Harry reading from Agnes's ledger line by line while Carmen asks questions. The dramatic engine is 'lecture with Q&A' — Harry delivers figures, Carmen reacts, Harry delivers more. Carmen's role is passive audience. While the information itself is compelling (the specificity of who gets what and why is one of the novel's strengths), the scene lacks dramatic pressure, conflict, or decision. Compare with the chapter 16 confrontation, where backstory exposition is embedded in a scene with real stakes. This chapter also establishes a template — Harry-as-information-deliverer, Carmen-as-recipient — that recurs in chapters 16 and 18, making the novel's revelation architecture feel single-geared.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1974-2020: restructure the financial reveal so that Carmen is an active participant rather than a passive audience. Strategy option A: Carmen discovers the financial architecture by reading Agnes's notebooks herself, with Agnes or Harry intervening only when she misreads something — this makes Carmen the investigator rather than the student. Strategy option B: embed the financial details in a scene with operational pressure — the reveal happens while the group is preparing for a handoff or dealing with a supply problem, so the numbers emerge through action rather than lecture. Strategy option C: build a disagreement into the scene — Carmen challenges the allocation (why does Marcus get 30%? why isn't more going to Burt's care?) and the financial details emerge through argument rather than presentation. Any strategy should preserve the specific dollar amounts and distribution details, which are the chapter's greatest strength.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter word count is >= 4000 while preserving heading contract.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add causally necessary scene material that strengthens the chapter engine, increases pressure, and lands the planned state shift without filler.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This is a dark comedy about elderly agency, institutional failure, and moral compromise in a setting most fiction treats as a punchline or a tragedy but never both. The risks the novel must take: render the comedy of octogenarians as pharmaceutical distributors without condescending to them or turning them into lovable rogues; make Carmen's escalating complicity fully legible without moralizing about it; show the bodily realities of aging — incontinence, tremor, cognitive decline, falls, the smell of a body that doesn't leave a building — with unsentimental specificity; render the economic desperation of both healthcare workers and uninsured buyers without turning either into social-issue abstractions; give the moral ambiguity of the ring genuine weight by refusing to resolve it into either 'they were wrong' or 'they were heroes.' The novel must trust readers to sit with complexity. When in doubt between too raw and too safe, choose raw. A nursing home that smells like potpourri is a nursing home written by someone who's never been in one.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism for bodily functions, aging, economic shame, institutional dysfunction, or the realities of nursing home care is a craft failure. The novel's comedy and humanity depend on rendering what polite fiction glosses over: what it smells like to clean someone who can't clean themselves, what it costs to count coins for a bus fare, what oxycodone sells for per milligram on the secondary market, what a concussion looks like on an 88-year-old skull, what it sounds like when someone with Parkinson's tries to hold a coffee cup steady, what Burt's dementia actually looks like up close. If the prose flinches from these specifics, the novel loses its authority.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the default for all dialogue. Uncontracted forms appear only for emphasis, character-specific formality (Agnes's low contraction level), Carmen under extreme stress reverting to formal speech, or deliberate register choices. In a contemporary New Jersey nursing home, stiff diction is the anomaly, not the norm.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity scenes with reflective interiority. Vary scene count: single-scene chapters for maximum compression, three-scene chapters for range. Comic chapters (3, 9) should feel structurally different from quiet character studies (7, 8, 12, 15), which should feel different from crisis chapters (11, 16, 17). Follow a confrontation with a reflection, an escalation with a domestic scene, a comic set piece with an elegiac one. If two consecutive chapters share the same rhythm, one needs redesigning.
prose_style_profile.diction: Grounded and specific. Drug names (oxycodone, alprazolam, hydroxychloroquine), nursing procedures (med pass, pill count, charting, fall assessment), body realities (incontinence, tremor, laceration, scalp bleed). No purple prose, no literary performance. The style should feel like someone who works with their hands is telling you the truth about what she saw.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, varying by POV character. Carmen chapters are warm and sensory, anchored in the body and the immediate environment. Harry chapters are precise and calculating, with attention to numbers and angles. Agnes chapters are clinical and compressed. Lola chapters are winding and emotionally layered, with embedded memories. Earl chapters are spare and weighted, with silence doing as much work as language. Marcus chapters are kinetic, with physical movement and code-switching. Tammy chapters are flat and observational, her trained eye cataloguing what doesn't add up.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied by chapter and POV. Carmen chapters favor medium sentences with occasional long runs when she's anxious. Harry chapters are short and clipped. Comic set pieces (chapters 3 and 9) move between fast inventory and slow domestic beats. Quiet chapters (7, 12, 15) slow down and let space onto the page. No chapter should feel rhythmically identical to the one before it.
